name: New American Funding - Colorado Springs, CO
description_short: New American Funding Colorado Springs is a mortgage lender offering conventional, FHA, and VA loans with rates as low as 3% down. They provide financing from loan origination through closing.
description_long:
New American Funding's Colorado Springs branch operates under the leadership of founders Rick and Patty Arvielo, who established the company with a mission to help homebuyers across the country achieve homeownership. The branch is located at 102 South Tejon Street, Suite 1100 in Colorado Springs and serves both local and national clients seeking mortgage financing solutions.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of mortgage products including conventional loans (3% down), FHA loans (3.5% down), and VA loans with no down payment required for eligible veterans and active duty military. They also provide NAF Cash, a service that allows buyers to make competitive cash offers and close in as little as seven days without selling a current home first. Additional offerings include refinancing options, mortgage calculators, and amortization tools to help borrowers understand their options.

New American Funding distinguishes itself through its Pathway to Homeownership initiative, which provides up to $6,000 in non-repayable assistance to qualified first-time homebuyers in select areas. This program can be combined with other down payment assistance programs. The company emphasizes quick closing timelines and maintains a 4.9/5 rating based on 269,421 reviews, suggesting strong customer satisfaction. Their team includes dedicated underwriters and funders positioned as specialists in efficient loan processing.

While the company demonstrates solid customer ratings and multiple loan product options, the website provides limited specific information about interest rates, fees, closing costs, or detailed underwriting requirements. The availability of the Pathway to Homeownership is limited to "select areas," which may exclude some Colorado Springs residents. As a branch operation, borrowers should verify whether all advertised programs and rates apply specifically to the Colorado Springs location versus national offerings.
address: 102 S Tejon St Office 1133, Colorado Springs, CO 80903

company_info.headquarters: 102 S Tejon St Office 1133, Colorado Springs, CO 80903

pros: Offers VA loans with no down payment requirement and special terms for veterans and active duty military
Provides Pathway to Homeownership program with up to $6,000 in non-repayable down payment assistance for qualified first-time buyers in select areas
NAF Cash service enables competitive cash offers and closing in as little as seven days without requiring home sale contingency
Conventional loans available with down payments as low as 3%, providing flexibility for various buyer profiles
High customer satisfaction rating of 4.9/5 based on 269,421 reviews
Free mortgage calculators including affordability, refinance, and amortization tools available on website
Full-service mortgage process from loan origination through closing with dedicated underwriting team
cons: Pathway to Homeownership assistance limited to "select areas," unclear which Colorado Springs neighborhoods qualify
Website does not display current interest rates, APRs, or specific fee structures for loan products
Limited transparency about underwriting requirements, credit score minimums, or debt-to-income ratio thresholds
NAF Cash service and some programs may not be available in all states (New York explicitly noted as unavailable)
As a branch location, unclear which services and rates are specific to Colorado Springs versus corporate-wide offerings
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking down payment assistance through the Pathway to Homeownership program
Veterans and active duty military members looking for VA loans with zero down payment options
Homebuyers preferring to close quickly and make competitive cash offers through NAF Cash service
Colorado Springs residents seeking conventional or FHA loans with down payments as low as 3-3.5%
diagnosis: New American Funding Colorado Springs is best for first-time homebuyers and veterans seeking straightforward mortgage financing with lower down payment requirements, backed by strong customer reviews. The main caveat is that specific rates, fees, and program eligibility details are not transparently displayed on the website, requiring prospective borrowers to contact the branch directly for pricing and to confirm which programs apply to their situation.
services: Conventional mortgage loans with down payments as low as 3%
FHA mortgage loans with 3.5% down payment option
VA mortgage loans with no down payment for veterans and active duty military
NAF Cash service for competitive cash offers and rapid closing (as little as 7 days)
Pathway to Homeownership program (up to $6,000 non-repayable assistance for first-time buyers in select areas)
